indicator_text: Lobbying report — quarterly disclosure record filed under the LDA (entity = LobbyingReport object, filterable by year and other columns)
scoring_rule: N/A (data field, not a scored indicator). Primary unit of LDA disclosure; filterable by year and other columns; linked many-to-many to LobbyingIssue (issue codes lobbied) and to LobbyingSpecificIssue (free-text issue narratives).
source_quote: reports = LobbyingReport.query.filter_by(year=2011) ... returns all lobbying reports filed in 2011 papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:239-243; "based on the universe of lobbying reports filed under the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995" :9-10
notes: LDA-direct field. Quarterly cadence: "lobbying report will almost always only mention legislation from a single Congress" :81-82. Federal (1995-) only.

indicator_text: Specific-issue narrative text — the free-text section of an LDA report describing what the lobbying activity addressed; full-text indexed in Whoosh for searching
scoring_rule: N/A. Indexed via lda/ix_utils.py issue_search; supports phrase and bag-of-words queries.
source_quote: the text of each lobbying report specific issue ... duplicated in a separate full-text index database :253-256; "issue_search ... return_objects ... make_phrase" :257-264
notes: LDA-direct field; LobbyView enrichment = full-text index over it.

indicator_text: Issue-area code lobbied on (e.g., 'TRADE (DOMESTIC/FOREIGN)') — many-to-many link from a lobbying report to one or more standardized LDA issue codes
scoring_rule: N/A. Filter expression shown: `LobbyingReport.issues.any(LobbyingIssue.code.in_(['TRADE (DOMESTIC/FOREIGN)']))` returning 52,418 reports.
notes: LDA-direct field (LDA standardized issue list); LobbyView normalizes into many-to-many table.

indicator_text: Filing year of a lobbying report; used heuristically to bound candidate Congress when bill numbers lack annotation
scoring_rule: N/A. Used as anchor: "consider a range of candidate Congresses extending backwards from the Congress containing the year that the lobbying report was filed."
source_quote: By default, we consider the three preceding Congresses :63-64; "If no bill having the same number exists in the entire range of Congresses we consider, we simply guess that the bill comes from the Congress of the year the lobbying report was filed." :77-78
notes: LDA-direct field; load-bearing input to bill-disambiguation pipeline.

indicator_text: Congressional bill record, primary key of form `{congress}_{billnumber}` (e.g., `110_HR7311`); linked to titles, terms, CRS summary, introduction date
scoring_rule: N/A. Primary key encoding: "the id is a string of the form 110 HR7311, where 110 is the number of the congress to which this bill belongs, and HR7311 is the bill number."
source_quote: the id is a string of the form 110 HR7311 ... To retrieve a particular bill by its id, use the following snippet: b = Bill.query.get('110_HR7311') :201-204
notes: External-data field (Congress.gov / CRS); LobbyView enrichment = linked into the lobbying database via bill-matching pipeline (the paper's headline contribution).

indicator_text: Date a bill was introduced (Bill.introduced field)
scoring_rule: N/A. Standard Field-typed attribute on Bill; "we can get the date that the bill was introduced with b.introduced".
source_quote: we can get the date that the bill was introduced with b.introduced, get its CRS summary text with b.summary :206-207

indicator_text: CRS (Congressional Research Service) summary text of a bill; full-text indexed in Whoosh
scoring_rule: N/A. Full-text indexed via bills/ix_utils.py summary_search; supports phrase queries (e.g., 'uruguay round' as phrase vs bag-of-words).
source_quote: Two types of data items are duplicated in a separate full-text index database to facilitate more efficient searching: the CRS summary text of each bill, and the text of each lobbying report specific issue. :253-256; "summary_search ... make_phrase=True" :283-292
notes: Congressional-data field; LobbyView enrichment = full-text index used to retrieve topic-specific bill subsets (example: trade-related bills via phrase queries).

indicator_text: Bill title — one-to-many: each bill has one or more BillTitle objects
scoring_rule: N/A. Used both for storage and as the index against which lobbying-report text is matched in step 4 of the bill-detection pipeline.
source_quote: titles = OneToMany('BillTitle') :216; "Bills are sometimes only referred to by titles or alternate names. To account for this, we clean and tokenize the specific issue sections of the lobbying report, and perform a text matching operation against a table of bill titles." :84-87
notes: Congressional-data field; load-bearing for bill-title-matching enrichment.

indicator_text: Bill subject-matter term — many-to-many between Bill and Term, supporting topic-based bill grouping
scoring_rule: N/A. Many-to-many: "looking at b.terms gives all of the terms that that bill is classified under, and for a term t, looking at t.bills gives all bills under that term."
notes: Congressional-data field (likely sourced from CRS subject-term taxonomy).

indicator_text: Inferred link between a lobbying report and one or more bills it discusses — produced by the bill-detection pipeline (regex bill-numbers + cosine-similarity Congress disambiguation + propagation + title matching + range expansion)
scoring_rule: N/A. Not a single field but the central enrichment: edges from {LobbyingReport, LobbyingSpecificIssue} to Bill objects.
source_quote: LobbyView bridges two distinct observable political behaviors with regard to congressional bills: (1) sponsorship by politicians, and (2) reported lobbying by interest groups. :11-12
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment (the paper's headline). Method: 5-step pipeline detailed in §2 — Bill Number Search (regex), Congress Identification (cosine similarity over bag-of-words around the bill mention vs candidate Congress bills' bag-of-words), Congress Propagation (majority vote within report), Bill Title Search (text match against title table), Bill Range Expansion (e.g., H.R. 4182-4186 → 4182, 4183, 4184, 4185, 4186, default range ≤10).

indicator_text: Cosine-similarity score between bag-of-words representation of text around a bill mention and bag-of-words representation of each candidate Congress's same-numbered bill; argmax selects the assigned Congress
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment. Default range = 3 preceding Congresses including the report-year Congress. NLP pipeline: tokenization + stopword filtering. Falls back to report-year Congress if no candidate bill exists.

indicator_text: Firm-level identifier — standardized identifier for the lobbying entity across reports
scoring_rule: N/A. Paper says LobbyView "allows researchers to identify political actors and their lobbying activities based on standardized firm- and industry-level identifiers". Detailed firm-disambiguation methodology is forward-promised in the paper but not described in this draft ("we will show 1) how we disambiguate interest group names using natural language processing (NLP) as well as collaborative filtering").
source_quote: identify political actors and their lobbying activities based on standardized firm- and industry-level identifiers to facilitate systemic research on lobbying and merging with external datasets :12-14; "from database.firms.models import *" :187
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment (NLP + collaborative filtering name disambiguation; external-dataset merge). Underlying raw input = LDA registrant/client names.

indicator_text: Industry classification (NAICS, with NAICS2 used for grouping in herfindahl.py)
scoring_rule: N/A. NAICS used to group firms into industries for Herfindahl-index computation; SIC available as alternative ("python -m hfcc.herfindadd namerica naics -s 1996 -e 2011").
source_quote: sorted by industry as identified by NAICS2 :307-308; "using NAICS (rather than SIC), starting from 1996 and ending in 2011" :320-322
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment, but classification itself is sourced externally (Compustat merge). Available range demonstrated: 1996-2011.

indicator_text: Firm-level financial fields from Compustat, joined onto the lobbying record by firm identifier
scoring_rule: N/A. "Adds firm-level compustat financial data" to the herfindahl.py output.
source_quote: Pulls firm-level financial and LDA report information from lobbying database :305-306; "Adds firm-level compustat financial data" :316
notes: External-dataset merge enrichment (Compustat). Not in raw LDA disclosures.

indicator_text: Herfindahl–Hirschman index for the industry to which a firm belongs (industry-level concentration measure)
scoring_rule: N/A. Computed from firm-level lobbying / financial data within an industry.
source_quote: Herfindahl index measures the levels of competition among firms (clients) within the same industry. :301-302; "Computes Herfindahl indices ... Outputs rows with firm information (sorted by industry as identified by NAICS2), industry Herfindahl index, and lobbying information for firm." :306-308
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment. One CSV file generated per year per classification system (NAICS or SIC).

indicator_text: Boolean flag: did this firm lobby on at least one trade issue (in a given year)
scoring_rule: N/A. "Adds indicator showing whether firm lobbied on at least one trade issue" — derived from issue codes + specific-issue text.
source_quote: Adds indicator showing whether firm lobbied on at least one trade issue :314-315
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment, illustrating the type of derived per-firm flags the database supports. Trade-specific because of the project's substantive focus (trade liberalization).

indicator_text: API providing programmatic bulk download of LobbyView data in standard formats — infrastructure layer, not a data field per se but a load-bearing access mechanism
scoring_rule: N/A. Mentioned in abstract as a deliverable.
source_quote: we develop an API (Application Programming Interface) that enables researchers to bulk download the massive amount of unstructured data in standard formats so that they can conduct further analyses using preferred statistical software. :14-17
notes: LobbyView-added infrastructure. Not described in further detail in this paper draft.

indicator_text: Full-text (Whoosh) indices over CRS bill summaries and lobbying-report specific-issue text — supports phrase and bag-of-words queries
scoring_rule: N/A. Two indices: bills/ix_utils.py and lda/ix_utils.py; helper get_bill_specific_issues_by_titles searches for all titles of a bill in specific-issue texts.
source_quote: Two types of data items are duplicated in a separate full-text index database to facilitate more efficient searching: the CRS summary text of each bill, and the text of each lobbying report specific issue. :253-256; "get bill specific issues by titles, which is a simple special case of issue_search that searches for all of the titles of a particular bill in the specific issues, used in the database construction process to find the bills mentioned by title in specific issues." :266-269
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment infrastructure. Used internally for bill-title matching and externally for researcher queries.
